items.q: What is the key difference between mitosis and meiosis?
items.correct: Mitosis produces two genetically identical diploid cells; meiosis produces four genetically different haploid cells (gametes).
items.misconception: Mitosis and meiosis both produce gametes; they are basically the same.
items.random_wrong: Mitosis happens in plants and meiosis happens in animals.
items.why: The similar names lead to confusion; students mix up which process produces gametes vs body cells.

items.correct: An undifferentiated cell capable of dividing to produce more stem cells or differentiating into specialised cell types.
items.misconception: Stem cells are taken only from embryos and cannot be found in adults.
items.random_wrong: Stem cells are simply cells that haven't grown big enough yet.
items.why: Media coverage of embryonic stem cells overshadows adult stem cells, so students think 'stem cell' = 'embryo cell'.

items.q: What is the key structural difference between prokaryotic and eukaryotic cells?
items.correct: Eukaryotes have a membrane-bound nucleus and membrane-bound organelles; prokaryotes do not.
items.misconception: Prokaryotes have no DNA — that's why they have no nucleus.
items.random_wrong: Prokaryotes are always smaller than 1 micrometre and have no cytoplasm.
items.why: Students see 'no nucleus' and think 'no DNA', missing that prokaryotes still have DNA — it's just free in the cytoplasm.

items.q: Does the electrical impulse jump directly across a synapse?
items.correct: No — the impulse triggers release of a neurotransmitter chemical that diffuses across the gap to the next neurone.
items.misconception: The electrical signal jumps straight across the synapse gap.
items.random_wrong: The two neurones touch and pass current directly.
items.why: Students imagine a spark crossing the gap, but transmission across a synapse is chemical, which is why synapses ensure one-way signalling.

items.correct: No — mitosis produces two identical body cells for growth and repair; gametes are made by meiosis.
items.misconception: Mitosis produces sperm and egg cells.
items.random_wrong: Mitosis produces four different cells each time.
items.why: Students confuse the two divisions; meiosis halves the chromosome number to make varied gametes, while mitosis keeps the number the same.

items.q: Which neurone connects sensory and motor neurones in a reflex arc?
items.why: It's Relay. “Sensory” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: How many cells are produced by one round of mitosis?
items.why: The real figure is 2. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
